title: Executive Summary
content:
RTX closes 2025-12-31 with $88.6B of revenue (9.7% YoY) and $6.73B of net income (41.0% YoY). The period ended with an operating margin of 10.5% and a net margin of 7.6%, which frames how much of the reported scale translated into retained profitability. Revenue and earnings improved together, indicating that the growth story carried through to reported profitability.

Operating cash flow reached $10.6B (47.6% YoY), and after $2.63B of capital spending the business produced $7.94B of free cash flow (75.1% YoY). Cash generation exceeded reported earnings, a sign that the period converted accounting profit into liquidity with relatively little friction. Capital spending absorbed 3.0% of revenue, which helps frame how much internal cash flow remained available after reinvestment.

Current liquidity of 1.03x and debt-to-equity of 0.51x point to a balance sheet that is neither clearly stressed nor clearly overcapitalized.

The dashboard uses the same row order across reports, and values the filing does not disclose remain explicitly marked rather than being hidden.
*Asset Turnover: Asset turnover uses average assets for each annual period only when the adjacent annual balance is available.*
*Book Value Per Share: Book Value Per Share uses period-end shares outstanding.*
*Earnings Per Share (Diluted): Earnings Per Share (Diluted) uses diluted weighted-average shares from the income statement.*
*Return on Assets: ROA uses average assets for each annual period only when the adjacent annual balance is available.*
*Return on Equity: ROE uses average equity for each annual period only when the adjacent annual balance is available.*

title: Trend Analysis
content:
Across the annual periods shown here, Free Cash Flow provides the clearest recurring pattern, with the latest value at $7.94B and a compound annual growth rate of 17.6%. The multi-year progression is steady enough to suggest an improving operating profile.

The multi-year trend is positive, but the pace is moderate rather than transformative. Cash generation remained constructive, with $10.6B of operating cash flow translating into $7.94B of free cash flow. Balance-sheet pressure should be read through both leverage (0.51x debt-to-equity) and liquidity (1.03x current ratio), because those measures do not always point to the same conclusion about financial flexibility.

title: Risk Factors
content: No single balance-sheet or earnings issue dominates the reporting period used in this note. The main areas to monitor remain profitability at 10.5% and free cash flow at $7.94B, because those lines usually determine whether the balance sheet is strengthening or merely holding steady.

title: Analyst Notes
content:
RTX's profile does not reduce to a single standout strength or weakness; the more relevant question is how the income statement, cash flow statement, and balance sheet interact. That matters because leverage (0.51x debt-to-equity), liquidity (1.03x current ratio), and free cash flow ($7.94B) need to be read together rather than in isolation.

This note is anchored to the annual period ending 2025-12-31, with the prior period set at 2024-12-31. Metrics without the required statement support remain blank rather than estimated, so the analysis stays tied to the filing evidence rather than to gap-filled assumptions.
